title: Flaky Tangy Uruguayan Parsley Flatbread
description: A comforting, crisp flatbread infused with fresh parsley and a bright lemony tang, perfect for a light lunch or snack.
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine the flour, salt, sugar, and baking soda.
Create a well in the center and pour in the warm water and 2 tbsp of olive oil.
Mix until a shaggy dough forms, then knead on a lightly floured surface for about 5 minutes until smooth and elastic.
Place the dough in an oiled bowl, cover with a damp cloth, and let it rise for 20 minutes at room temperature.
Preheat a cast‑iron skillet or non‑stick pan over medium heat; lightly oil the surface.
Divide the dough into four equal portions and roll each into a thin oval, about 10‑inch wide.
Brush each flatbread with a little olive oil, then sprinkle the chopped parsley, lemon zest, and a pinch of salt evenly over the surface.
Cook each flatbread for 2–3 minutes per side, or until golden and flaky, turning once; while still hot, drizzle a thin layer of lemon juice over the top of each.
Serve warm, optionally garnished with extra parsley leaves.
